schema_diagram.description: Entity-Relationship diagram showing database schema and relationships
schema_diagram.content:
erDiagram
    CONSTRUCTORS ||--o{ CONSTRUCTORSTANDINGS : "has"
    RACES ||--o{ CONSTRUCTORSTANDINGS : "has"
    CONSTRUCTORS ||--o{ CONSTRUCTORRESULTS : "has"
    RACES ||--o{ CONSTRUCTORRESULTS : "has"
    DRIVERS ||--o{ LAPTIMES : "has"
    RACES ||--o{ LAPTIMES : "has"
    DRIVERS ||--o{ PITSTOPS : "has"
    RACES ||--o{ PITSTOPS : "has"
    CIRCUITS ||--o{ RACES : "has"
    SEASONS ||--o{ RACES : "has"
    CONSTRUCTORS ||--o{ QUALIFYING : "has"
    DRIVERS ||--o{ QUALIFYING : "has"
    RACES ||--o{ QUALIFYING : "has"
    DRIVERS ||--o{ DRIVERSTANDINGS : "has"
    RACES ||--o{ DRIVERSTANDINGS : "has"
    STATUS ||--o{ RESULTS : "has"
    CONSTRUCTORS ||--o{ RESULTS : "has"
    DRIVERS ||--o{ RESULTS : "has"
    RACES ||--o{ RESULTS : "has"

    CONSTRUCTORSTANDINGS {
        int constructorStandingsId PK
        int raceId FK
        int constructorId FK
        float points
        int position
        text positionText
        int wins
    }
    CIRCUITS {
        int circuitId PK
        text circuitRef
        text name
        text location
        text country
        float lat
        float lng
        int alt
        text url
    }
    SEASONS {
        int year PK
        text url
    }
    CONSTRUCTORS {
        int constructorId PK
        text constructorRef
        text name
        text nationality
        text url
    }
    CONSTRUCTORRESULTS {
        int constructorResultsId PK
        int raceId FK
        int constructorId FK
        float points
        text status
    }
    DRIVERS {
        int driverId PK
        text driverRef
        int number
        text code
        text forename
        text surname
        datetime dob
        text nationality
        text url
    }
    LAPTIMES {
        int raceId PK,FK
        int driverId PK,FK
        int lap PK
        int position
        text time
        int milliseconds
    }
    PITSTOPS {
        int raceId PK,FK
        int driverId PK,FK
        int stop PK
        int lap
        text time
        text duration
        int milliseconds
    }
    RACES {
        int raceId PK
        int year FK
        int round
        int circuitId FK
        text name
        datetime date
        text time
        text url
    }
    STATUS {
        int statusId PK
        text status
    }
    QUALIFYING {
        int qualifyId PK
        int raceId FK
        int driverId FK
        int constructorId FK
        int number
        int position
        text q1
        text q2
        text q3
    }
    DRIVERSTANDINGS {
        int driverStandingsId PK
        int raceId FK
        int driverId FK
        float points
        int position
        text positionText
        int wins
    }
    RESULTS {
        int resultId PK
        int raceId FK
        int driverId FK
        int constructorId FK
        int number
        int grid
        int position
        text positionText
        int positionOrder
        float points
        int laps
        text time
        int milliseconds
        int fastestLap
        int rank
        text fastestLapTime
        text fastestLapSpeed
        int statusId FK
    }

circuits.name: circuits
circuits.description: The circuits table stores information about racing circuits worldwide, including their locations across 15 countries, with the highest concentration in the USA.

country_values.content: USA=11(15%), France=7(10%), Spain=6(8%), UK=4(6%), Portugal=3(4%), Japan=3(4%), Italy=3(4%), Germany=3(4%), Canada=3(4%), Belgium=3(4%), Austria=3(4%), South Africa=2(3%), Brazil=2(3%), UAE=1(1%), Turkey=1(1%)

constructorResults.name: constructorResults
constructorResults.description: The constructorResults table records the points awarded to Formula 1 constructors in each race, with most entries indicating no points earned (0) and a small fraction marked with status 'D' for disqualification or other non-standard outcomes.

points_meaning.content: Points awarded to constructors in races based on finishing position; 0 indicates no points earned (e.g., did not finish in point-scoring positions)

constructorStandings.name: constructorStandings
constructorStandings.description: The constructorStandings table tracks the cumulative standings of Formula 1 constructors across races and seasons, including their positions, points, and number of wins.

quality_issues.description: TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 11819/11836 non-empty)

constructors.name: constructors
constructors.description: The constructors table stores information about Formula 1 constructor teams, including their nationalities, with a majority being British, reflecting the historical dominance of UK-based teams in the sport.

nationality_values.content: British=85(40.87%), American=39(18.75%), Italian=29(13.94%), French=12(5.77%), German=10(4.81%), Japanese=5(2.40%), Swiss=4(1.92%), South African=3(1.44%), Dutch=3(1.44%), Russian=2(0.96%), Malaysian=2(0.96%), Canadian=2(0.96%), Spanish=1(0.48%), Rhodesian=1(0.48%), New Zealand=1(0.48%)

driverStandings.name: driverStandings
driverStandings.description: The driverStandings table records the cumulative points, positions, and performance metrics for drivers across races in a motorsport season.

quality_issues.description: TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 31577/31578 non-empty)

drivers.name: drivers
drivers.description: The drivers table stores comprehensive information about racing drivers, including their nationalities, with a focus on historical representation across various countries.

nationality_values.content: British=162(23.5%), American=157(22.8%), Italian=99(14.4%), French=72(10.5%), German=49(7.1%), Brazilian=31(4.5%), Argentine=24(3.5%), Swiss=23(3.3%), South African=23(3.3%), Belgian=23(3.3%), Japanese=19(2.8%), Dutch=17(2.5%), Australian=17(2.5%), Spanish=15(2.2%), Austrian=15(2.2%)

quality_issues.description: Contains 1 empty string values in addition to NULLs

lapTimes.name: lapTimes
lapTimes.description: The lapTimes table records detailed lap-by-lap timing and position data for racing events, enabling performance analysis of drivers across individual laps.

quality_issues.description: TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 400524/400524 non-empty)

pitStops.name: pitStops
pitStops.description: The pitStops table records detailed information about each pit stop during Formula 1 races, including the stop sequence and duration in seconds.

duration_meaning.content: Duration of the pit stop in seconds, stored as TEXT with up to 3 decimal places (e.g., "22.838")

stop_values.content: 1=first pit stop(44%), 2=second pit stop(35%), 3=third pit stop(16%), 4=fourth pit stop(4%), 5=fifth pit stop(1%), 6=sixth pit stop(<1%)

quality_issues.description: TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 5815/5815 non-empty)
TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 5815/5815 non-empty)

qualifying.name: qualifying
qualifying.description: The qualifying table captures each driver's performance during Formula 1 qualifying sessions, including their car number, lap times in Q1, Q2, and Q3, and their final qualifying position.

number_meaning.content: Car number assigned to a driver for the race weekend; used for identification on track and in timing systems

position_meaning.content: Qualifying position in the session, where 1 = fastest qualifier (pole position), higher numbers indicate slower qualifying times

q1_meaning.content: Lap time recorded by driver in Qualifying Session 1 (Q1), formatted as M:SS.mmm (minutes:seconds.milliseconds)

q2_meaning.content: Lap time recorded by driver in Qualifying Session 2 (Q2), formatted as M:SS.mmm (minutes:seconds.milliseconds)

q3_meaning.content: Lap time recorded by driver in Qualifying Session 3 (Q3), formatted as M:SS.mmm (minutes:seconds.milliseconds)

quality_issues.description: TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 6858/6858 non-empty)
TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 3367/3367 non-empty)
Contains 23 empty string values in addition to NULLs
TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 2004/2004 non-empty)
Contains 28 empty string values in addition to NULLs

races.name: races
races.description: The races table stores information about individual racing events, including their identifiers, names, dates, and associated circuits or locations.

quality_issues.description: TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 232/232 non-empty)

results.name: results
results.description: The `results` table records detailed outcomes of Formula 1 races, including driver finishing positions, starting grid positions, points awarded, and race status codes for each entry.

grid_meaning.content: Starting position on the race grid; 1= pole position, higher numbers = further back on the grid

points_meaning.content: Points follow Formula 1 scoring: 25=1st, 18=2nd, 15=3rd, 12=4th, 10=5th, 9=6th, 8=7th, 7=8th, 6=9th, 5=10th; 0=non-point finish; historical/rare values (e.g., 50, 36) from older seasons

position_meaning.content: Finishing position in the race; 1=winner, higher numbers = lower finish; NULL or non-numeric cases handled by positionText

quality_issues.description: TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 4994/4994 non-empty)
TEXT field storing numeric values (100% numeric, 4994/4994 non-empty)

seasons.name: seasons
seasons.description: The seasons table stores a list of distinct seasons, likely used to categorize or reference time periods across other related data.

status.name: status
status.description: The status table stores a list of possible status values used across the system to represent the current state of various entities.
